agentFile.format: Markdown with YAML frontmatter; filename (sans .md) becomes the agent id/name
agentFile.bodyIs: The markdown body after the frontmatter is the agent system prompt/instructions (alternative to the `prompt` field)

description.description: Brief explanation of what the agent does and when to use it. Used for routing/@mention discovery.

mode.description: Interaction mode. primary = main assistant cycled via Tab; subagent = specialized, invoked automatically or via @mention; all = eligible as both.

model.description: Model override for this agent in provider_id/model_id form.

temperature.description: Sampling randomness; lower = more deterministic, higher = more creative.

prompt.description: Custom system prompt for this agent. Can reference an external file via {file:./prompts/x.txt} (path relative to config). If omitted, the markdown body is used.

tools.allowed: { <toolname>: true|false }, e.g. write: false, bash: false
tools.description: DEPRECATED. Enable/disable tools for the agent. true is equivalent to {"*":"allow"}, false is equivalent to {"*":"deny"}. Superseded by `permission`.

permission.allowed: per-key value "allow" | "ask" | "deny", or a pattern-map of glob->action; keys include read, edit, bash, glob, grep, list, task, webfetch, websearch, lsp, skill, external_directory, todowrite (set partly unverified)
permission.description: Fine-grained per-tool gating (replaces `tools`). "ask" prompts for approval, "allow" runs without approval, "deny" disables the tool. Keys are matched as wildcard patterns against the underlying tool name (built-ins, custom tools, and MCP tools).

disable.description: When true, the agent is disabled and not loaded.

steps.description: Maximum agentic iterations (legacy alias maxSteps, deprecated).

bashInjection.description: Injects the stdout of the given shell command into the command prompt/template at run time. Documented for command files (.opencode/commands/*.md).

fileInjection.description: Includes the contents of the referenced file into the command prompt/template. Documented for command files.

argumentTokens.description: $ARGUMENTS expands to all arguments passed to the command; $1/$2/$3 expand to individual positional arguments. Scope: command files.

toolReference.description: Tools are enabled/disabled and gated per agent via the deprecated `tools` map or the current `permission` map. Permission keys are wildcard patterns matched against tool names (built-ins, custom tools in ~/.config/opencode/tool, and MCP tools).

mcpReference.token: {file:./path} for prompt files; MCP servers configured in opencode.json `mcp` section (type: local|remote)
mcpReference.description: MCP servers are declared in opencode.json under `mcp`. type "local" requires `command` (argv array) with optional `environment`, `enabled`, `timeout`; type "remote" requires `url` with optional `headers`, `oauth` (object {clientId,clientSecret,scope} or false), `enabled`, `timeout`. MCP-provided tools are then gated by the agent `permission` wildcard keys.

variableTemplating.description: {file:./path} embeds the contents of an external file; primarily used for the `prompt` field (system prompt) in agent files, with the path relative to the config file location. Argument/bash/file tokens above apply to command templates.

agentMention.description: Subagents (mode: subagent or all) can be invoked from a primary agent session via @mention; agents may also be invoked automatically. The `hidden` field can hide an agent from @ autocomplete.
